channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fired or frustrated federal workers may have eager employers waiting for them in Minnesota
item.description: For federal workers, the past few months have been filled with uncertainty as the Trump administration pushes ahead with efforts to reduce the size of government agencies. But whether they are forced or choose to leave, those workers may find employers eager to hire them.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Measuring the cost of extending Trump's tax cuts becomes a flashpoint in Congress
item.description: An obscure bookkeeping matter has become the latest flashpoint in Congress as Democrats accuse Republicans of violating Senate norms.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 things to know about Republicans’ new blueprint to pass Trump's agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday rolled out a 70-page blueprint to advance President Trump’s tax agenda, along with directions that could pave the way for more than a trillion dollars in cuts to government spending.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: EXCLUSIVE VIDEO: AOC refuses to say whether Democrats' rhetoric is responsible for Tesla attacks
item.description: Democrat Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez refused to answer whether Democrats' inflammatory rhetoric against Elon Musk is linked to attacks against Tesla owners and dealers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate Dems foreshadow more forced votes to block Trump's emergency orders
item.description: Senate Democrats may move to challenge additional Trump emergencies and force floor votes on them, Sen. Tim Kaine suggested at a press conference on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump announces $100M deal with law firm tied to Doug Emhoff, Jan. 6 House panel
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday announced his administration struck a deal with a law firm with ties to former second gentleman Doug Emhoff and the House panel that investigated the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol as Trump targets major firms for retribution.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs could hurt New York's Canadian tourism
item.description: Sen. Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y., said New York state businesses are concerned about Canadian relations affecting tourism.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Skiba, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Biden's top health official, Xavier Becerra, enters the race to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Biden administration Health Secretary Xavier Becerra on Wednesday became the latest Democrat to join the crowded field seeking to become California’s next governor, a contest that could be dramatically reshaped if former Vice President Kamala Harris becomes a candidate.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem attorneys general use new media to mount Trump resistance
item.description: Court proceedings might often unfold behind closed doors, but the Democratic attorneys general challenging President Trump’s expansive agenda want to bring their work to your feed.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Republicans win Florida special elections in Trump strongholds by narrower margins than in 2024
item.description: ORMOND BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Republicans Jimmy Patronis and Randy Fine won special elections Tuesday in two Florida congressional districts, bolstered by President Donald Trump’s endorsement to fill vacant seats in reliably Republican strongholds.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces sweeping new tariffs to promote US manufacturing, risking inflation and trade wars
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced far-reaching new tariffs on nearly all U.S. trading partners — including a 34% tax on imports from China and 20% on the European Union, among others.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CCSD opposes school choice bill, while lawmakers say it empowers families
item.description: The Clark County School District is against a bill that would let parents enroll their children at any public school regardless of where they live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health announces layoffs amid federal funding cuts
item.description: The agency issued layoffs and separation notices to 170 employees whose positions were supported by federal grants that have now been terminated.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: New Jersey Sen. Booker presses his marathon speech against Trump's agenda past 20 hours and counting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Sen. Cory Booker held the Senate floor with a marathon speech that lasted all night and into Tuesday afternoon in a feat of endurance to show Democrats’ objections to President Donald Trump’s sweeping actions.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Republican Jimmy Patronis wins Matt Gaetz's Florida District 1 seat: DDHQ
item.description: Valimont raised almost $6.5 million compared to Patronis' $2.1 million, according to Federal Elections Commission filings.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Yale professor leaving US: Trump launching 'brutal attack' on freedom
item.description: A professor at Yale University said Monday he would be leaving his post and the country over the Trump administration’s “brutal attack” on free speech.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Green card holders, travelers caught in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: A lawful permanent resident who has lived in the U.S. for 50 years was detained because of a decades-old conviction amid tougher immigration enforcement at airports and border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams' lawyer urges ‘prompt resolution’ ahead of election deadline
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – With a filing deadline approaching in New York City’s upcoming mayoral election, Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer has urged a judge to "promptly" issue a decision in the mayor’s bribery case. Petitions are due this week for candidates to appear on a party's primary ballot.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9
